items.description:

3. **Scraper errors**: Is the scraper producing 0 jobs (all sources failing)?

items.title: [Bug]: Operator Manual is out of sync with current config and workflow behavior
items.description:
### Bug Type
Other

### Description
`docs/wiki/Operator-Manual.md` contains stale instructions that no longer match repository behavior.

Examples:
- It references `filters.exclude_keywords`, but current config uses `filtering.exclusion_signals`.
- It suggests tuning thread pools directly in `scripts/update_jobs.py`, while worker-pool behavior is now configurable via `config.yml` (`worker_pools`).
- It references JobSpy location/config patterns that no longer match current configuration shape.
- Several workflow guidance notes are stale relative to `.github/workflows/update-jobs.yml`.

This creates contributor confusion and increases maintenance error risk.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open `docs/wiki/Operator-Manual.md`.
2. Compare recipe/config field names against `config.yml`.
3. Compare workflow instructions against `.github/workflows/update-jobs.yml`.
4. Observe mismatches between documented and actual behavior.

### Additional Context
Candidate source-of-truth files for alignment:
- `docs/wiki/Operator-Manual.md`
- `config.yml`
- `.github/workflows/update-jobs.yml`
- `scripts/update_jobs.py`

items.title: [aw] Daily Backlog Burner failed
items.description:
### Workflow Failure

**Workflow:** [Daily Backlog Burner](#)  
**Branch:** main  
**Run:** https://github.com/ambicuity/New-Grad-Jobs/actions/runs/23241125968


**🛡️ Protected Files**: The code push was refused because the patch modifies protected files (package manifests, agent instruction files, or repository security configuration). This protection guards against unintended supply chain changes.

**Blocked Operations:**
- `create_pull_request`: Cannot create pull request: patch modifies protected files (.github/workflows/ci.yml). Add them to the allowed-files configuration field or set protected-files: fallback-to-issue to create a review issue instead.

To review and apply these changes manually, configure `protected-files: fallback-to-issue` — the agent will create a review issue with instructions instead of blocking:
```yaml
safe-outputs:
  create-pull-request:
    protected-files: fallback-to-issue
```



### Action Required

**Option 1: Debug using any coding agent**

Use this prompt with any coding agent (GitHub Copilot, Claude, Gemini, etc.):

````
Debug the agentic workflow failure using https://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/gh-aw/main/debug.md

The failed workflow run is at https://github.com/ambicuity/New-Grad-Jobs/actions/runs/23241125968
````

**Option 2: Assign this issue to Copilot**

Assign this issue to Copilot using the `agentic-workflows` sub-agent to automatically debug and fix the workflow failure.

**Option 3: Manually invoke the agent**

Debug this workflow failure using your favorite Agent CLI and the `agentic-workflows` prompt.

- Start your agent
- Load the `agentic-workflows` prompt from `.github/agents/agentic-workflows.agent.md` or <https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/main/.github/agents/agentic-workflows.agent.md>
- Type `debug the agentic workflow daily-backlog-burner failure in https://github.com/ambicuity/New-Grad-Jobs/actions/runs/23241125968`

> [!TIP]
> To stop a workflow from creating failure issues, set `report-failure-as-issue: false` in its frontmatter:
> ```yaml
> safe-outputs:
>   report-failure-as-issue: false
> ```

items.title: [aw] No-Op Runs
items.description:
This issue tracks all no-op runs from agentic workflows in this repository. Each workflow run that completes with a no-op message (indicating no action was needed) posts a comment here.
📘 What is a No-Op? A no-op (no operation) occurs when an agentic workflow runs successfully but determines that no action is required. For example: - A security scanner that finds no issues - An update checker that finds nothing to update - A monitoring workflow that finds everything is healthy These are successful outcomes, not failures, and help provide transparency into workflow behavior.
🎯 How This Helps This issue helps you: - Track workflows that ran but determined no action was needed - Distinguish between failures and intentional no-ops - Monitor workflow health by seeing when workflows decide not to act
📚 Resources - [GitHub Agentic Workflows Documentation](https://github.com/github/gh-aw)
> [!TIP] > To stop a workflow from posting here, set `report-as-issue: false` in its frontmatter: > ```yaml > safe-outputs: > noop: > report-as-issue: false > ``` --- > This issue is automatically managed by GitHub Agentic Workflows. Do not close this issue manually. > > **No action to take** - Do not assign to an agent. > - [x] expires on Apr 9, 2026, 9:31 PM UTC
